title: PM Approval Scoped Decision History
objective: Narrow PM approval decision-history reads so route-scoped approval detail screens use existing entity_id and limit query support instead of eagerly loading full decision history, while preserving the explicit Decision History screen and avoiding backend, schema, auth, ingress, or mutation changes.
orchestration_inputs: PM Lane 012 remains the deployment gate for hosted mutation-seam parity and live PM proof.
Read-only scout 019e2c88-c9e7-7813-82a9-48e124ca3819 confirmed the approval route was eagerly fetching full decision history and that the mutation seam already supports repeated entity_id plus limit.
Coordinator execution selected a frontend-only route-derived read narrowing change plus focused browser proof, keeping the product slice separate from hosted Render remediation.
constrained_outputs: Approval detail screens build a scoped decision-history endpoint for task-review, wp-review, snapshot-review, and escalations when detailId is present.
Scoped approval history reads use entity_id=<detailId> and limit=25.
Queue and unresolved-detail screens skip decision-history reads because they do not render history.
The explicit history screen preserves the existing full decision-history read.
Focused Playwright proof captures decision-history requests and asserts escalation and snapshot deep links use scoped reads with no unscoped decision-history fetch on those screens.
Existing approval tracer and schedule handoff behavior remains covered by the same focused smoke.
validation_steps: CI=1 corepack pnpm --dir C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform --filter @apex/operations-web exec playwright test tests/browser-shell.approval-context.smoke.spec.ts
validation_results: focused approval-context Playwright proof passed 2/2 after rebuilding operations-web
initial focused Playwright attempts before the rebuild exercised stale built output and correctly failed the new scoped-history assertions; the rebuilt bundle passed
sql_permission_note: Packet 2026-05-15-pm-lane-014 is frontend-only and does not authorize SQL writes, schema migration, live data mutation, auth widening, ingress widening, schedule mutation, assignment mutation, Vercel promotion, or Render deployment action.
